<<<KOT:seminiferous>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"This adjective describes a biological property of reproductive tissues defined by their role in meiosis and gamete production. The underlying mechanisms — germ-cell division, gamete formation, and sex-specific reproductive anatomy — require technical biological grounding (cells, meiosis, spermatozoa) that lies outside the available prime lexicon and catalog. It belongs to the specialized biology sector."}
<<<END:seminiferous>>>>

<<<KOT:shelflike>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"This adjective expresses resemblance to a shelf through a comparative-form modifier. While shelf itself might be definable, the relational predicate of resemblance or structural analogy — requiring LIKE or similar comparative operators applied across object-types — exceeds what the prime lexicon and frame grammar are designed to ground independently. The concept is fundamentally definitional by reference to another artefact, not by intrinsic everyday sensory or functional properties."}
<<<END:shelflike>>>>
